# Ark Nova card database â€” operating manual

**To answer a card question: clone this repo and run SQL through `scripts/query.py`. Do not try to answer from this file alone. Do not WebFetch `cards.jsonl` and parse it by hand â€” the data is meant to be queried, not read.**

```
git clone https://github.com/torbjorv/ark-nova-for-llms.git && cd ark-nova-for-llms
python scripts/query.py "<SQL>"
```

`scripts/query.py` loads the card data into an in-memory SQLite table named `cards`, runs your SQL, and writes the result as JSONL on stdout â€” one JSON object per row, with JSON-typed columns emitted as parsed JSON. Use `python scripts/query.py - < query.sql` for long queries.

The rest of this file is the SQL schema you write against (`cards` table), the SQLite JSON1 idioms for the array/object columns, the closed enums, and the small set of non-obvious answering rules.

## Examples

```
python scripts/query.py "SELECT id, name, appeal FROM cards WHERE type = 'animal' ORDER BY appeal DESC LIMIT 3"

python scripts/query.py "SELECT id, name FROM cards \
  WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each(continents) WHERE value = 'europe') \
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each(categories) WHERE value = 'bear')"
```

If you can't run shell commands yourself, write SQL against the schema below and ask the user to run it through `query.py`.

## Schema

Single table `cards`. Every row has every column.

### Scalar columns

| column | type | notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | TEXT | e.g. `AN-401`, `MW-544`. The prefix indicates the printing source (base box vs marine box); use the `set` column for deck-membership filters. |
| `name` | TEXT | |
| `type` | TEXT | enum (see below) |
| `rock_icons` | INTEGER | enclosure rock-icon count, â‰Ą 0 |
| `water_icons` | INTEGER | enclosure water-icon count, â‰Ą 0 |
| `size` | INTEGER \| NULL | base footprint for animals; NULL on non-animals |
| `appeal` | INTEGER \| NULL | typical on animals |
| `conservation_points` | INTEGER \| NULL | mostly on animals |
| `strength` | INTEGER \| NULL | sponsor strength; NULL on non-sponsors |
| `reputation_requirement` | INTEGER \| NULL | mostly on sponsors |
| `bonus_reward` | TEXT \| NULL | always-fires reward on play / on support â€” `"<N> reputation"` for animals' release-rep and release-CP rep; non-rep card-wide rewards on management-plan CPs (e.g. `"build 1 kiosk or pavilion"`) |
| `money_cost` | INTEGER \| NULL | on animals; NULL on sponsors / projects / final-scoring |
| `text` | TEXT | verbatim card text. Animals use `""` â€” their effects are encoded as tags, not prose |
| `notes` | TEXT \| NULL | e.g. Latin name |
| `standard_size` | INTEGER \| NULL | enclosure-fit fields below: NULL = animal cannot live there |
| `reptile_house_size` | INTEGER \| NULL | |
| `large_bird_aviary_size` | INTEGER \| NULL | |
| `petting_zoo_size` | INTEGER \| NULL | |
| `aquarium_size` | INTEGER \| NULL | |
| `large_reptile_house_size` | INTEGER \| NULL | |
| `reef_ability` | TEXT \| NULL | reef ability code (Marine Worlds) |
| `wave_icon` | INTEGER (0/1) | wave trigger icon (Marine Worlds); SQLite has no native BOOL |

### JSON-typed columns

Stored as JSON text; query with the JSON1 functions. **Duplicates encode multiplicity** in `continents`, `categories`, `abilities`, `requires`, `provides` â€” don't dedupe when counting icons.

| column | shape | example |
|---|---|---|
| `set` | array of TEXT | `["base","marine-worlds"]`, `["base"]`, `["marine-worlds"]` â€” see *Set semantics* below |
| `continents` | array of TEXT | `["europe"]`, `["africa","africa"]` |
| `categories` | array of TEXT | `["predator","predator"]` |
| `abilities` | array of TEXT | `["sprint"]` |
| `requires` | array of TEXT | `["bear","animals-ii"]` |
| `provides` | array of TEXT | `["science"]` |
| `triggers` | array of TEXT | `["ongoing","end"]` |
| `ability_levels` | object: tag â†’ INTEGER | `{"sprint": 3}` |
| `ability_targets` | object: tag â†’ TEXT | `{"iconic": "europe"}` |
| `tier_thresholds` | array of INTEGER | conservation-project / final-scoring tier thresholds (`[]` if no escalating ladder â€” see partnership / management-plan CPs) |
| `tier_rewards` | array of TEXT | conservation-project / final-scoring rewards per slot. Each entry is short text â€” `"<N> CP"` for plain rewards, e.g. `"2 CP + Hunter 1"` or `"2 CP + 2 rep"` when a slot grants more than CP. Same length as `tier_thresholds` when both are populated. |

Idioms:

- **Membership:** `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each(<col>) WHERE value = '<tag>')`
- **Occurrence count** (multiplicity): `(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM json_each(<col>) WHERE value = '<tag>')`
- **Array length:** `json_array_length(<col>)`
- **Object lookup:** `json_extract(ability_levels, '$.sprint')`
- **All distinct values:** `SELECT DISTINCT value FROM cards, json_each(cards.abilities)`

### Set semantics

`set` is a JSON array listing the deck configurations the card appears in (it is *not* a single product label). Values come from `{base, marine-worlds}`:

| `set` value | meaning |
|---|---|
| `["base", "marine-worlds"]` | base card with no marine errata â€” appears in pure-base **and** base+marine games |
| `["base"]` | base card replaced by a marine errata â€” only appears in pure-base games |
| `["marine-worlds"]` | either a new marine card or the marine errata replacement of a base card â€” only appears in base+marine games |

Filter idioms:

- *"In a base-only game"* / *"in the base set"* â†’ `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each("set") WHERE value = 'base')`
- *"In a base+marine game"* â†’ `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each("set") WHERE value = 'marine-worlds')`
- *"What does Marine Worlds add or change"* (new + errata) â†’ `NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each("set") WHERE value = 'base')`

Note: `set` is a SQLite reserved word â€” quote it as `"set"` in SQL.

### Enums

```
type:       animal | sponsor | conservation-project | final-scoring
continents: africa | americas | asia | europe | australia
categories: bear | bird | herbivore | petting-zoo | predator | primate | reptile | sea-animal
set:        base | marine-worlds   (each appears as one or more elements of the `set` array)
```

Tag values for `abilities`, `requires`, `provides`, and `triggers` are a closed vocabulary defined in `ABILITIES.md`. **Don't invent tags.** If a user references a tag not in `ABILITIES.md`, tell them so and suggest the closest real one. To list the live vocabulary in-database: `SELECT DISTINCT value FROM cards, json_each(cards.abilities)` (and the same for the other three columns).

## How to answer queries

1. **Filter on structured columns first.** Use `text` only as a fallback for rulings / interaction flavor, and say so when you do.
2. **Animal categories live in `categories`, not `abilities`.** "Europe + bear" is `EXISTS (... continents = 'europe')` AND `EXISTS (... categories = 'bear')`.
3. **Sea animals** are filtered as `categories` membership of `'sea-animal'` (and/or `aquarium_size IS NOT NULL`). There is no "marine" enclosure-icon â€” `rock_icons` and `water_icons` stay separate integers.
4. **Enclosure fit:** to find animals placeable in a given enclosure kind, filter on the matching `*_size` column being non-null.
5. **Report counts with the matching `id â€” name` list** so the user can verify.
6. **Nulls / `[]` / `{}` mean "does not apply"**, not "unknown." Don't guess at blanks; say "not captured in this dataset."
